div.p: that he has seen the sign of the Son of Man as foretold in the 24th chapter of Matthew.1

The slanderous allusion of a “seraglio” like the Grand Turk, which the editor applies to me, he may take to himself, for, “out of the abundance of the heart the mouth speaketh.”2 Every honest man who has visited the city of Nauvoo since it existed, can bear record of better things, and place me in the front ranks of those who are known to do good for the sake of goodness, and show all liars, hypocrites3 and abominable creatures that, while vice sinks them down to darkness4 and woe, virtue exalts me and the Saints to light and immortality.5

The editor, as well as some others, “thinks that Joe Smith has his match at last,” because Mr. Redding thinks that he has seen the sign of the Son of Man. But I shall use my right, and declare that, notwithstanding Mr. Redding may have seen a wonderful appearance in the clouds one morning about sunrise (which is nothing very uncommon in the winter season) he has not seen the sign of the Son of Man, as foretold by Jesus;6 neither has any man, nor will any man, until after the sun shall have been darkened and the moon bathed in blood;7 for the Lord hath not shown me any such sign;8 and as the prophet saith, so it must be—“Surely the Lord God will do nothing, but He revealeth His secret unto His servants the prophets.” (See Amos 3:7.)9 Therefore, hear this, O earth:10 The Lord will not come to reign over the righteous, in this world, in 1843, nor until everything for the Bridegroom is ready.11

The battle of Gog and Magog will be after the millennium.12 The remnant of all the nations that fight against Jerusalem13 were commanded to go up to Jerusalem to worship in the millennium.14 (March 4, 1843.) DHC 5:298.
div.div: Giving Blessings a Tax on Strength
div.p: Elder Jedediah M. Grant enquired of me the cause of my turning pale and losing strength last night while blessing children.15 I told him that I saw that Lucifer16 would exert his influ-
